content.full_text:
California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
J. Kenneth Brown 
Brown, Winfield and Canzoneri 
Attorneys at Law 
300 South Grand Avenue 
suite 1500 
Los Angeles, CA 
90071-3125 
Dear Mr. Brown: 
September 21, 1988 
Re: 
Your Request for Informal 
Assistance 
Our File No. I-88-32l 
You have requested advice regarding the mass mailing 
provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").!! as 
recently amended by proposition 73. 
As city attorney for 
several cities, you have advised your clients that under the 
Commission's emergency regulation 18901 (copy enclosed), 
newsletters may include the names of the present city council 
only in the standard letterhead or logotype. 
The newsletters 
may not contain pictures, signatures, or other references to 
members of the city council. 
This is to confirm that your advice is an accurate 
explanation of the emergency regulation . 
.!! 
Government Code sections 81000-91015. 
All statutory 
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise 
indicated. 
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code 
of Regulations Section 18000, et seq. 
All references to 
regulations are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code 
of Regulations. 
428 J Street, Suite 800 • 
P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660 


J. Kenneth Brown 
September 21, 1988 
Page -2-
The regulation provides: 
(b) 
A mailing will not be deemed to be 
prohibited by Section 89001 if the mailing meets all 
of the following criteria: 
(1) 
It is mailed by an elected officer's 
agency; 
(2) 
The stationery, forms and envelopes 
used for the mailing are the official stationery, 
forms and envelopes of the agency; and 
(3) 
The elected officer's name appears, if 
at all, only on the standard letterhead or 
logotype of the stationery, forms or envelopes 
and there are no other references to the officer, 
including his or her photograph or signature, in 
the mailing. 
Regulation 18901(b) 
The Commission is presently in the process of developing a 
permanent regulation which may modify this advice. 
We will 
include your name on the mailing list for information on this 
topic. 
If you have any questions, please contact me. 
Sincerely, 
~~~ 
JGM:ld 
Enclosure 


J. KENNETH BROWN 
THOMAS F. WINF;ELD 
ANTHONY CANZONERI 
VICKI E. LAND 
..JAMES C. CAMP 
STEPHANIE ROSE SCHER 
STEVEN ABRAM 
THOMAS D. GREEN 
DENNIS S, ROY 
MARK W. STERES 
DEBRA SPOTTS BLUM 
MARK GARRETT 
MAUREEN M. MURANAKA 
WENDY J, PHILLIPPAY 
CHRISTOPHER P. DOWICZ 
KATHARINE A. MILLER 
K. AN DREW KENT 
..JEFFREY LEVITAN 
BROWN, WINFIELD & CANZONERI 
rNCORPORATED 
ATTORNEYS AT LAW 
CALIFORNIA PLAZA 
300 SOUTH GRAND AVENUE, SUITE 1500 
LOS ANGELES, CALIFORNIA 90071-3125 
TELEPHONE: 
(2)3) 687-2100 
August 9, 1988 
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION 
428 J. Street, suite 800 
Sacramento, California 
95814 
Re: 
Newsletters 
Gentlemen: 
TELECOPI ER: 
(213) 687-2149 
FILE NO: 
DIRECT DIAL NO: 
This office represents the cities of Cerritos, 
La 
Canada Flintridge, Monterey Park, 
Norwalk, 
San Dimas and San 
Gabriel, 
which have from time to time published newsletters 
containing matters of general information to the community, along 
with schedules of activities, summaries of city council actions, 
etc. 
In each 
instance, 
these cities have included in the 
newsletter letterhead identification of the present members of 
the city council. 
In view of the emergency regulation which the 
Fair Political Practices commission adopted at its meeting on 
July 26th, 
and the earlier July 1st advise letter, 
we 
are 
advising 
our 
cities that 
they 
may 
prepare 
their 
upcoming 
ner,.,lslet.ters in compliance with this emergency regulation and 
include only the names of the present members of the city council 
in the standard letter or logotype with no pictures, signatures, 
or other reference to the members of the city council. 
In this 
regard, I expect that this reflects the practice of other public 
agencies. 
Since these cities are preparing newsletters which 
include information regarding scheduled upcoming activities and 


FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION 
August 9, 1988 
Page 2 
events, I am advising the clients of this office that they can 
rely upon this emergency regulation. 
If for any reason these 
cities should not rely upon the emergency regulation, I 
would 
appreciate your advising me. 
JKB:dsd 
cc: 
Mr. Gaylord F. Knapp, city Manager, Cerritos 
Mr. Donald H. Otterman, city Manager, La Canada Flintridge 
Mr. David P. Bentz, Interim city Manager, Monterey Park 
Mr. Richard R. Powers, city Manager, Norwalk 
Mr. Robert L. Poff, city Manager, San Dimas 
Mr. Robert D. Clute, city Administrator, San Gabriel

sections.question: You have requested advice regarding the mass mailing provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act"), as recently amended by proposition 73. As city attorney for several cities, you have advised your clients that under the Commission's emergency regulation 18901 (copy enclosed), newsletters may include the names of the present city council only in the standard letterhead or logotype. The newsletters may not contain pictures, signatures, or other references to members of the city council.
sections.conclusion: This is to confirm that your advice is an accurate explanation of the emergency regulation. The regulation provides that a mailing will not be deemed prohibited if it is mailed by an elected officer's agency using official stationery and the elected officer's name appears only on the standard letterhead or logotype with no other references to the officer, including photograph or signature.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether city newsletters may include the names of city council members only in standard letterhead or logotype without pictures, signatures, or other references under the Political Reform Act as amended by Proposition 73 and the Commission's emergency regulation 18901.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC confirms that the requestor's advice to clients is accurate: city newsletters may include council member names only in standard letterhead or logotype, with no pictures, signatures, or other references, in compliance with emergency regulation 18901.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document includes both incoming request letter (dated August 9, 1988) and FPPC response letter (dated September 21, 1988). Question and conclusion sections extracted from response letter. The document clearly shows the full advisory exchange with the FPPC response confirming the requestor's legal interpretation.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: You have requested advice regarding the mass mailing provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act"), as recently amended by proposition 73. As city attorney for several cities, you have advised your clients that under the Commission's emergency regulation 18901 (copy enclosed), newsletters may include the names of the present city council only in the standard letterhead or logotype. The newsletters may not contain pictures, signatures, or other references to members of the city council.

CONCLUSION: This is to confirm that your advice is an accurate explanation of the emergency regulation. The regulation provides that a mailing will not be deemed prohibited if it is mailed by an elected officer's agency using official stationery and the elected officer's name appears only on the standard letterhead or logotype with no other references to the officer, including photograph or signature.

embedding.summary: The FPPC responds to a city attorney's request for confirmation that newsletters complying with emergency regulation 18901 may include city council member names only in standard letterhead or logotype without additional references. The Commission confirms the requestor's interpretation is accurate.
